# DeltaSharp secret-scanner test fixture — STORY-00.3.1 (#107).
#
# These are NON-FUNCTIONAL, obviously-fake values used ONLY to prove that
# tools/security/secret-scan.py still detects secret-shaped strings on every run. They are
# not real credentials and grant access to nothing. DO NOT add real secrets to this file.
#
# This file is covered by an unexpired allowlist entry in
# tools/security/secret-scan-allowlist.json (with scope/reason/owner/expiry), so the
# scanner REPORTS it as an expected match without failing CI. If that allowlist entry
# expires, the scanner will flag this file — that is the intended review trigger.

# AWS access key id — the canonical AWS-documentation example value (recognized broadly as
# a non-secret example, so it does not trip GitHub-native push protection).
aws_access_key_id = AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE

# DeltaSharp project canary — a stable, obviously-fake, non-provider token so the fixture
# always yields a deterministic, push-protection-safe detection.
deltasharp_canary = DELTASHARP_TEST_SECRET_000000000000000000000000
